assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table lines 27-31 shows all 5 criteria as PASS: (1) single license filter via validate_license_param and is_in filter, (2) comma-separated filter via comma splitting producing OR semantics, (3) 400 validation via spdx::Expression::parse rejection, (4) pagination integration confirmed with total==5 and items.len()==2, (5) unchanged response shape PaginatedResults<PackageSummary>. Detailed evidence in criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: 'Scope Containment | PASS | PR modifies exactly the files specified in the task: list.rs, service/mod.rs (modified), tests/api/package.rs (created)'. Lines 35-39 provide file-by-file comparison: PR files match task files exactly with 'No out-of-scope files. No unimplemented files.'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 11: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No reviews or comments exist on the PR'. Line 12: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report files: report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md. All are verification/analysis documents. No source code files (.rs, .toml, etc.) were created or modified in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'All checks pass. The implementation correctly adds a license filter... No issues requiring attention.' and 'This report was generated by the verify-pr skill for eval purposes.' No merge command, merge API call, or auto-merge action is present anywhere in the outputs. The report presents findings without taking merge action.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 16: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, API keys, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines'. Lines 43-51 detail the security scan: 'Scanned all added lines across 3 files. No matches for: Hardcoded passwords or secrets, API keys or tokens, Private keys or certificates, Environment/configuration files with secrets, Cloud provider credentials, Database credentials with embedded passwords.'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 20: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | All test files are newly created; no existing tests modified or deleted'. Lines 64-67: 'Classification: ADDITIVE. tests/api/package.rs is a newly created file. No existing test files were modified or deleted. All test changes are purely additive, introducing 4 new test functions covering the new license filter functionality.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table (lines 9-21) contains all required verdicts: Scope Containment (PASS), Diff Size (PASS), Commit Traceability (N/A) from Intent Alignment domain; Sensitive Patterns (PASS) from Security domain; CI Status (PASS), Acceptance Criteria (PASS), Verification Commands (N/A) from Correctness domain; Test Quality (PASS), Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE) from Style/Conventions domain. All 9 verdict rows are present.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Beyond the summary table, report.md contains: (1) Scope Containment section (lines 35-39) with file-by-file comparison listing exact paths for PR files vs task files; (2) Security Scan section (lines 43-51) describing scanning 'all added lines across 3 files' with 6 specific pattern categories checked; (3) Acceptance Criteria Detail table (lines 23-31) with per-criterion code-level evidence citing specific functions, SQL constructs, and test names; (4) Test Quality section (lines 55-61) listing all 4 test functions with their doc comments and assessing test patterns. Additionally, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md provide multi-paragraph detailed analysis per acceptance criterion with Implementation Evidence and Test Evidence subsections.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 11-12: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No reviews or comments exist on the PR' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'. These two verdicts appear at the top of the summary table (lines 11-12) before the domain-specific verdicts (lines 13-21), structurally separated from the domain analysis sections. The reasons given match the assertion exactly: N/A due to no review comments and N/A due to no sub-tasks.
